title: The Third Sunday of Advent
context:
# Temporal Context Snapshot: Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## December 11, 2022 - "The Third Sunday of Advent"

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Third Sunday of Advent, also known as Gaudete Sunday (from the Latin "rejoice"), falls in the second week of the Advent season leading toward Christmas. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings typically included Isaiah 61:1-4, 8-11 (focusing on good news to the oppressed), Psalm 126 or Luke 1:46b-55, 1 Thessalonians 5:16-24 (rejoice, give thanks, hold fast to what is good), and John 1:6-8, 19-28 (John the Baptist's witness). This Sunday emphasizes joy and the accelerating anticipation of Christ's coming, marked liturgically by the lighting of the third Advent candle.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in transition following General Convention 2022 in Baltimore (July), where significant resolutions on LGBTQ+ inclusion and affirming same-sex marriage had passed. The Diocese of Colorado, led by Bishop Kimberly Lucas (elected 2019), was navigating these national church developments. Saint John's Cathedral itself, historically a progressive voice in the diocese, would have been engaged with these post-Convention conversations about implementation of the church's more inclusive marriage canons that would take effect in January 2023.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing unseasonable mild weather in early December 2022, with temperatures well above normal for the season—a pattern consistent with broader climate trends. City politics centered on budget discussions and implementation of new Mayor Mike Johnston's first major initiatives following his election in May 2022. The downtown Capitol Hill area, where the Cathedral is located, continued recovery and revitalization efforts post-pandemic, with retail and restaurant activity slowly increasing.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Biden signed the landmark Inflation Reduction Act's implementation phase, with major clean energy provisions taking effect; the administration was also releasing the National Climate Assessment emphasizing urgency of climate action. The midterm election aftermath dominated political discourse, with Republicans having gained control of the House in November and beginning congressional investigations into the January 6th Capitol riot. The Supreme Court's 2022 term continued generating national division over abortion rights and gun regulations following the June Dobbs decision.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's war in Ukraine entered its tenth month with winter conditions intensifying the humanitarian crisis; the U.S. and allies were increasing weapons shipments including advanced air defense systems. The COP27 climate conference had concluded in Egypt just weeks prior (November 6-20) with a contentious agreement on climate reparations and loss-and-damage funding for vulnerable nations. China was experiencing rare anti-government protests over strict COVID lockdown policies, with citizens openly challenging Xi Jinping's zero-COVID approach.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was deeply polarized and fatigued, with economic anxiety about inflation, political exhaustion, and climate anxiety forming a subdued backdrop to the holiday season. Major League Baseball's offseason was dominated by free agent negotiations and the anticipation of the World Series aftermath (Houston Astros' first championship). The cultural zeitgeist carried remnants of pandemic-era fragmentation while people attempted to return to "normal" holiday traditions, creating a tension between nostalgia and uncertainty that Advent's themes of hope and waiting would have resonated with particularly well.
